title: Exocarpos rolfeanus
intro: Exocarpos rolfeanus, also known as the Philippine sandpaper tree, is a small evergreen tree native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Santalaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of E. rolfeanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used to create topical creams and ointments for wound care and skin conditions.
